channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Stephen Colbert exits 'The Late Show' with a mix of defiance and celebration
item.description: Colbert has approached the last weeks of his show on his own terms, with A-list guests like Tom Hanks, David Letterman and David Byrne — but the guests for tonight's finale remain a mystery.
item.content:encoded: Colbert has approached the last weeks of his show on his own terms, with A-list guests like Tom Hanks, David Letterman and David Byrne — but the guests for tonight's finale remain a mystery.
item.dc:creator: David Bianculli

item.title: 'It's one of my dreams,' Rose Byrne says of her comic turn on Broadway
item.description: Byrne is one of the few actors to receive both an Oscar and a Tony nomination in the same year —for the indie film If I Had Legs I'd Kick You, and for Fallen Angels on Broadway.
item.content:encoded:
.'/>

Byrne is one of the few actors to receive both an Oscar and a Tony nomination in the same year —for the indie film If I Had Legs I'd Kick You, and for Fallen Angels on Broadway.

(Image credit: Andreas Rentz)
item.dc:creator: Ann Marie Baldonado

item.title: After the sirens: Lebanon's first responders swing between duty and grief
item.description: Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly 3,000 people have been killed and nearly 1 million have been displaced the war in southern Lebanon began in March. Nearly 400 have been killed since a ceasefire began in April.

(Image credit: Diego Ibarra Sánchez)
item.dc:creator: Diego Ibarra Sánchez

item.title: An unlikely opera for America's birthday summons dissonance and harmony
item.description: When John Cage composed an opera commemorating the American bicentennial audiences walked out. Now, it's being reinterpreted by new artists in a Detroit Opera production, as the nation turns 250.
item.content:encoded:
Apartment House 1776 which runs May 21 to 24.'/>

When John Cage composed an opera commemorating the American bicentennial audiences walked out. Now, it's being reinterpreted by new artists in a Detroit Opera production, as the nation turns 250.

(Image credit: Austin T. Richey)
item.dc:creator: Neda Ulaby

item.title: Sisters reunite in Ali Smith's 'Glyph,' bringing light after the darkness of 'Gliff'
item.description: The author restores balance in the homophones with her latest novel; both stories are thought-provoking, although somewhat less beguiling than her usual fare.
item.content:encoded: The author restores balance in the homophones with her latest novel; both stories are thought-provoking, although somewhat less beguiling than her usual fare.
item.dc:creator: Heller McAlpin

item.title: The history of Kermit the Frog, an American icon
item.description: A beloved cultural icon who's bright green? Kermit the Frog is also quintessentially American.
item.content:encoded: A beloved cultural icon who's bright green? Kermit the Frog is also quintessentially American.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: After the Kars4Kids ad is banned in California, we check in on nostalgic jingles past
item.description: A California judge has given Kars4Kids 30 days to either take its ads off the air or update them to disclose its affiliations to a Jewish charity based in the Northeast.
item.content:encoded: A California judge has given Kars4Kids 30 days to either take its ads off the air or update them to disclose its affiliations to a Jewish charity based in the Northeast.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: The Justice Department gives Trump an unprecedented settlement
item.description: D.O.J. gave Trump and his family immunity from tax audits and set up a $1.8 billion fund for victims of "government weaponization." Former government lawyer Andrew Weissmann explains the settlement.
item.content:encoded: D.O.J. gave Trump and his family immunity from tax audits and set up a $1.8 billion fund for victims of "government weaponization." Former government lawyer Andrew Weissmann explains the settlement.
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: Having trouble focusing on your book? Try immersive reading
item.description: Reading text of a book while listening to the audiobook is gaining steam among online book communities.
item.content:encoded: Reading text of a book while listening to the audiobook is gaining steam among online book communities.
item.dc:creator: Chloee Weiner

item.title: Every season of 'Survivor,' ranked worst to best, by NPR's resident superfan
item.description: Survivor is a sport, a metaphor for society and yes, entertainment. As its 50th season ends, NPR critic Stephen Thompson ranked every season so far.
item.content:encoded: Survivor is a sport, a metaphor for society and yes, entertainment. As its 50th season ends, NPR critic Stephen Thompson ranked every season so far.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson
